page: Microsoft Azure E96-48as_v4 is an EASv4-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs with 2.0 threads per core. The processor operates at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and is equipped with 32 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. This instance is configured with 672.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 14.0 GB per core. It includes 1376 GB of local storage and does not feature any GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 25%. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Geekbench ray tracing, and image processing also rank in the top 25%. Memory subsystem performance is highlighted by a PassMark memory score in the top 10%, although raw memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks fall within the middle 50%. For database and key-value workloads, PassMark database operations and Redis SET operations demonstrate average performance. Large-scale LLM inference shows a clear tradeoff, with prompt processing and text generation for small models scoring in the top 25%, while text generation for large 70B models drops to the bottom 10%.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E96-48as_v4 offers high memory density relative to its compute footprint, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require proportional scaling of vCPU counts. The inclusion of 1376 GB of local storage provides a substantial local cache or scratch space without requiring external storage attachments. This server type is suited for large-scale in-memory databases, memory-intensive data processing, enterprise applications, and multi-threaded decompression tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E96-48as_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at 3.525 GHz. It is equipped with 672.0 GB of RAM, providing a high ratio of 14.0 GB per core, alongside 1376 GB of local storage. Benchmark results show strong multi-core CPU performance and a top-tier PassMark memory score in the top 10%, though memory bandwidth and database operations remain in the average tier. The instance exhibits a performance tradeoff in LLM inference, where small model execution is strong but large 70B model text generation falls into the bottom 10%. This server is suited for memory-intensive workloads, in-memory databases, and multi-threaded data processing tasks that benefit from high memory density without requiring GPU acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96-48as_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs, 672 GB of RAM, and 1376 GB of local storage, delivering strong multi-core CPU and top-tier memory performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96-48as_v4 is a memory-optimized VM with 48 vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and 1376 GB local storage, built on AMD EPYC 7763 processors.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized virtual machine with dedicated AMD EPYC processing and substantial local storage for memory-bound enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores
High-density memory configuration of 672.0 GB RAM yielding 14.0 GB per core
Bundled 1376 GB of local storage for fast data access and caching
Top 10% PassMark memory score and top 25% multi-core CPU performance
Suited for in-memory databases, enterprise applications, and memory-bound data processing
